description: JSSE is a from-scratch JavaScript engine implemented in Rust. It currently passes 90,365 out of 91,986 test262 scenarios (98.24%), leaving 1,621 failing tests. This PRD defines the work required to close every remaining gap and reach 100% test262 compliance — forward progress only, no regressions.

userStories.title: Fix BigInt literal syntax validation (parser)
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, the parser must reject BigInt literals with exponent parts (e.g., `0e0n`, `1E1n`) as SyntaxError, and correctly handle legacy non-octal decimal integers in strict mode.
acceptanceCriteria: Parser rejects BigInt literals containing exponent parts (`0e0n`, `1e2n`, etc.) with SyntaxError
Parser rejects legacy non-octal decimal integers in strict mode (e.g., `08n`, `09n`)
test262/test/language/literals/bigint/ — all currently failing tests pass (~10 new passes)
test262/test/language/literals/numeric/ — legacy octal escape tests pass (~7 new passes)
No regressions in language/literals/ (542 total tests)

userStories.title: Fix destructuring assignment evaluation order
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, destructuring assignment targets (property references, computed keys) must be evaluated BEFORE consuming iterator values or accessing source properties, per the spec's step ordering in §13.15.5.
acceptanceCriteria: In iterator destructuring, target expressions (e.g., `a[key]`) are evaluated before `IteratorStep` calls
In keyed destructuring, target reference expressions are evaluated before `GetV` on the source
test262/test/language/expressions/assignment/ — evaluation-order tests pass (~21 new passes)
test262/test/language/statements/for-of/ — related destructuring order tests pass (~10 new passes)
test262/test/language/expressions/object/dstr/ — method destructuring default tests pass (~15 new passes)
test262/test/language/expressions/arrow-function/dstr/ — arrow destructuring tests pass (~5 new passes)
No regressions in language/expressions/assignment/ or language/statements/for-of/

userStories.title: Fix `with` statement scope semantics
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, the `with` statement must correctly create an Object Environment Record whose bindings shadow outer scope, with proper completion value handling and variable declaration hoisting through the `with` scope.
acceptanceCriteria: Property lookups through `with` object correctly shadow outer scope bindings
Completion values from empty `with` body return `undefined`
`eval()` inside `with` correctly interacts with the with-scope's Object Environment Record
Function declarations inside `with` hoist correctly
test262/test/language/statements/with/ — all failing tests pass (~26 new passes)
No regressions in language/statements/ suite

userStories.title: Fix generator `.return()` and `.throw()` control flow
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, calling `.return()` on a generator must execute `finally` blocks but NOT `catch` blocks, and calling `.throw()` must correctly propagate through try-catch-finally in all nesting combinations. This applies to both sync and async generators.
acceptanceCriteria: `generator.return(val)` executes finally blocks in nested try-finally contexts
`generator.return(val)` does NOT execute catch blocks
`generator.throw(err)` correctly propagates through nested try-catch-finally
Async generator `.return()` properly awaits finally block promises
test262/test/built-ins/GeneratorPrototype/ — all failing tests pass (~18 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/AsyncGeneratorPrototype/return/ — all failing tests pass (~14 new passes)
No regressions in generator/async-generator test suites

userStories.title: Fix `yield*` delegation with thenables and AsyncFromSyncIterator throw
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `yield*` delegating to an async iterator whose `next()`/`return()`/`throw()` returns a thenable (not a Promise) must wrap it via `PromiseResolve()`, invoking the `get then()` getter. The AsyncFromSyncIteratorPrototype `.throw()` method must correctly wrap sync iterator throw results.
acceptanceCriteria: `Await(thenable_obj)` checks for a callable `then` property and enqueues PromiseResolveThenableJob
`Get(resolution, "then")` throwing causes promise rejection, not uncaught error
AsyncFromSyncIteratorPrototype.throw() wraps sync results in promises correctly
test262/test/language/expressions/yield/ — yield* delegation tests pass (~16 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/AsyncFromSyncIteratorPrototype/throw/ — all failing tests pass (~18 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/AsyncFromSyncIteratorPrototype/ — remaining failures pass (~14 new passes)
No regressions in generator/async-generator suites

userStories.title: Fix `super` expression evaluation order
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `super.prop` and `super[expr]` must evaluate the super base reference BEFORE evaluating the property key or argument list, and `super()` call must evaluate the argument list BEFORE checking `IsConstructor`.
acceptanceCriteria: `super()` evaluates argument list before checking if super class is a constructor
`super[expr]` evaluates the super base before `ToPropertyKey(expr)`
Accessing `super.x` with uninitialized `this` throws ReferenceError at the correct point
test262/test/language/expressions/super/ — all failing tests pass (~18 new passes)
No regressions in language/expressions/super/

userStories.title: Fix optional chaining tail-call and edge cases
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, optional chaining expressions must correctly interact with tail call optimization (the `?.()` form should be eligible for TCO in tail position) and template literals after optional chains must be early errors.
acceptanceCriteria: TCO flag propagated correctly through optional chaining call expressions
Tagged template after optional chain is a SyntaxError (early error)
`this` preserved correctly in optional method calls (`obj?.method()`)
test262/test/language/expressions/optional-chaining/ — all failing tests pass (~14 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix `using`/`await using` remaining edge cases
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `using` and `await using` declarations must create fresh bindings per loop iteration, handle disposal ordering correctly in nested blocks, and integrate with for-of iteration semantics.
acceptanceCriteria: `using` in for-of creates a fresh binding per iteration
`await using` in for-of creates a fresh binding per iteration
Disposal ordering in nested try-finally-using blocks follows spec
test262/test/language/statements/using/ — all failing tests pass (~15 new passes)
test262/test/language/statements/await-using/ — all failing tests pass (~21 new passes)
No regressions in explicit resource management tests

userStories.title: Fix Promise combinator iterator protocol compliance
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `Promise.all`, `Promise.allSettled`, `Promise.any`, and `Promise.race` must correctly handle iterator protocol edge cases: IteratorClose on error, resolve element function ordering, species creation from different realms, and non-iterable argument TypeError.
acceptanceCriteria: When mapFn/element-access throws, IteratorClose is called before rejecting
Resolve element functions execute in correct order
Non-iterable arguments throw TypeError (not silently fail)
Cross-realm Promise species creation works correctly
test262/test/built-ins/Promise/ — all failing tests pass (~46 new passes)
No regressions in Promise tests

userStories.title: Implement resizable ArrayBuffer/SharedArrayBuffer compliance
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, resizable ArrayBuffers (with `maxByteLength` option) and growable SharedArrayBuffers must correctly handle `transfer()`, `transferToFixedLength()`, and `resize()`/`grow()` edge cases including detached buffer checks, MAX_BYTE_LENGTH tracking, and cross-type detection.
acceptanceCriteria: `transfer()` from resizable to resizable preserves maxByteLength
`transferToFixedLength()` from resizable produces a fixed-length buffer
`resize()` correctly handles out-of-bounds, zero-length, and max-byte-length cases
Detached buffer detection works correctly with resizable buffers
SharedArrayBuffer type checks reject ArrayBuffer and vice versa for resizable variants
test262/test/built-ins/ArrayBuffer/prototype/ — all failing tests pass (~42 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/SharedArrayBuffer/ — all failing tests pass (~24 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/DataView/prototype/ — resizable-related tests pass (~22 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix RegExp remaining edge cases
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, RegExp must handle remaining edge cases in `/u`/`/v` flag parsing, property escapes, exec method behavior, Symbol.replace/match/search integration, and Annex B legacy features.
acceptanceCriteria: RegExp `/u` and `/v` flag pattern validation rejects all spec-invalid patterns
RegExp property escapes handle all Unicode 17.0 categories
RegExp.prototype.exec correctly handles all edge cases (lastIndex, sticky, global)
Symbol.replace/match/search protocol compliance
test262/test/built-ins/RegExp/ — all failing tests pass (~58 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/RegExpStringIteratorPrototype/ — all failing tests pass (~12 new passes)
test262/test/annexB/built-ins/RegExp/ — legacy feature tests pass (~14 new passes)
No regressions in RegExp suite

userStories.title: Fix Array prototype remaining edge cases
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, Array prototype methods must handle all remaining edge cases: species creation, sparse array handling, length validation, and Proxy-aware iteration.
acceptanceCriteria: Array.prototype methods correctly handle species creation for subclasses
Sparse array methods respect holes vs undefined semantics
Array length property validation edge cases (ToLength, 2^32-1 boundary)
Array methods work correctly when `this` is a Proxy
test262/test/built-ins/Array/ — all failing tests pass (~58 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix Iterator helper edge cases
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, Iterator helper methods (concat, zip, zipKeyed, from) must handle all edge cases in the iteration protocol: error ordering, iterator cleanup on abort, value extraction, and GetMethod/GetIterator error propagation.
acceptanceCriteria: Iterator.zip handles iterator cleanup when one iterator throws
Iterator.zipKeyed handles poisoned iterators (getter throws)
Error propagation from GetIterator/GetMethod follows spec ordering
test262/test/built-ins/Iterator/ — all failing tests pass (~34 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix AsyncIteratorPrototype Symbol.asyncDispose
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `AsyncIteratorPrototype[Symbol.asyncDispose]` must call `.return()` if defined, wrap the result in a Promise, and return a fulfilled promise. The unwrap function must return undefined per spec.
acceptanceCriteria: `iter[Symbol.asyncDispose]()` calls `return()` method if present
Result is wrapped via PromiseResolve
Unwrap function returns undefined
Side effects from return method getter are observable
test262/test/built-ins/AsyncIteratorPrototype/Symbol.asyncDispose/ — all tests pass (~18 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix TypedArrayConstructors remaining internals
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, TypedArray exotic objects must implement `[[Delete]]` per §10.4.5.10 (return true for detached/OOB, false for valid index, OrdinaryDelete for non-canonical), and BigInt typed array constructors must handle ToBigInt coercion and ContentType mismatch.
acceptanceCriteria: `[[Delete]]` returns true for detached buffer cases
`[[Delete]]` returns true for out-of-bounds indices
`[[Delete]]` returns false for valid integer indices
Non-canonical index strings fall through to OrdinaryDelete
BigInt typed arrays use ToBigInt (not ToNumber) for element coercion
ContentType mismatch between BigInt/non-BigInt typed arrays throws TypeError
test262/test/built-ins/TypedArrayConstructors/ — all failing tests pass (~16 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix Function built-in edge cases
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `Function.prototype.bind()`, `Function.prototype.toString()`, and the Function constructor must handle all remaining edge cases including bound function length/name, toString representation, and constructor prototype behavior.
acceptanceCriteria: Bound function `.length` correctly computed (max(0, target.length - args.length))
Bound function `.name` is `"bound " + target.name`
`Function.prototype.toString()` handles all function forms (native, bound, arrow, etc.)
test262/test/built-ins/Function/ — all failing tests pass (~20 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix Temporal PlainYearMonth remaining failures
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, Temporal.PlainYearMonth must handle all remaining edge cases in cross-realm construction, calendar option processing, and property descriptor compliance.
acceptanceCriteria: PlainYearMonth cross-realm construction uses correct prototype
Calendar option edge cases handled correctly
Property descriptors on PlainYearMonth instances match spec
test262/test/built-ins/Temporal/PlainYearMonth/ — all failing tests pass (~42 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/Temporal/ — remaining failures pass (~24 new passes across other types)
No regressions in Temporal suite

userStories.title: Fix module namespace exotic object internals
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, module namespace objects must implement all exotic internal methods per §10.4.6: `[[GetOwnProperty]]`, `[[DefineOwnProperty]]`, `[[HasProperty]]`, `[[Get]]`, `[[Set]]`, `[[Delete]]`, `[[OwnPropertyKeys]]`, `[[PreventExtensions]]`, `[[IsExtensible]]`, and `[[SetPrototypeOf]]`.
acceptanceCriteria: `[[Get]]` returns live binding values (not snapshots) for exported names
`[[Get]]` throws ReferenceError for uninitialized bindings (TDZ)
`[[HasProperty]]` returns true for exported names, false for others
`[[Set]]` always returns false (namespace objects are immutable)
`[[Delete]]` returns true for non-exported properties
`[[OwnPropertyKeys]]` returns sorted exported names then symbols
`[[PreventExtensions]]` returns true (namespaces are already non-extensible)
`[[DefineOwnProperty]]` rejects all attempts
test262/test/language/module-code/namespace/internals/ — all tests pass (~36 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix module early errors and evaluation semantics
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, the module parser must detect all §16.2 early errors (duplicate exports, lexical/var conflicts, reserved identifiers in imports, `super`/`new.target` in module root) and module evaluation must follow the correct ordering for default exports, self-evaluation, and required-module sequencing.
acceptanceCriteria: Duplicate export names throw SyntaxError at parse time
Lexical + var binding name overlap in module scope throws SyntaxError
`arguments` and `eval` as import binding names throw SyntaxError
`super` and `new.target` in module root code throw SyntaxError
Module evaluation follows depth-first dependency order
Default export evaluation works for all forms (class, function, expression)
test262/test/language/module-code/ root-level early-error tests pass (~36 new passes)
test262/test/language/module-code/ eval-* tests pass (~32 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix top-level await syntax validation
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, the parser must correctly handle `await` as an expression in module code (where it's valid) and reject it in non-module contexts. The ~211 TLA syntax tests validate `await` grammar in all expression positions within modules.
acceptanceCriteria: `await expr` is valid as an expression in module top-level code
`await` is valid in all expression positions per spec (assignment, conditional, unary, etc.)
`for await...of` is valid in module top-level code
test262/test/language/module-code/top-level-await/syntax/ — all tests pass (~211 new passes)
test262/test/language/module-code/top-level-await/ — execution tests pass (~18 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix import attributes and JSON module imports
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `import ... with { type: 'json' }` syntax must be parsed and JSON modules must be loaded correctly with proper attribute validation, duplicate key detection, and namespace/named-binding restriction enforcement.
acceptanceCriteria: Import attributes (`with { key: 'value' }`) are parsed correctly
Duplicate attribute keys throw SyntaxError
JSON modules loaded via `with { type: 'json' }` expose a default export only
Named bindings from JSON modules throw SyntaxError
JSON module object is not extensible (frozen)
test262/test/language/module-code/import-attributes/ — all tests pass (~13 new passes)
test262/test/language/import/json-* — JSON module tests pass (~12 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix ambiguous star-export resolution
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `export * from` re-exports must correctly handle ambiguous bindings: when two star-exports provide the same name, the binding is ambiguous and must be excluded from the namespace object (not throw at link time).
acceptanceCriteria: Ambiguous re-exported names excluded from namespace objects
Named imports of ambiguous bindings throw SyntaxError
Complex re-export chains resolve correctly
test262/test/language/module-code/ambiguous-export-bindings/ — all tests pass (~9 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Implement Atomics multi-agent support
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `Atomics.wait()`, `Atomics.waitAsync()`, and `Atomics.notify()` must work with the `$262.agent` test harness to support multi-agent (multi-threaded) test scenarios. This requires implementing the Agent abstraction that allows test262 to spawn worker-like agents that share memory via SharedArrayBuffer.
acceptanceCriteria: `$262.agent.start(script)` spawns an agent that executes the given script
`$262.agent.broadcast(sab)` shares a SharedArrayBuffer with all agents
`$262.agent.getReport()` retrieves messages from agents
`$262.agent.sleep(ms)` pauses the calling agent
`$262.agent.monotonicNow()` returns a monotonic timestamp
`Atomics.wait()` blocks the calling agent thread (not main thread)
`Atomics.notify()` wakes blocked agents
`Atomics.waitAsync()` returns a promise that resolves when notified
test262/test/built-ins/Atomics/wait/ — all tests pass (~104 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/Atomics/waitAsync/ — all tests pass (~118 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/Atomics/notify/ — all tests pass (~32 new passes)
test262/test/built-ins/Atomics/ — remaining failures pass (~2 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Implement `import defer` (deferred module evaluation)
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `import defer * as ns from "module"` must parse and defer module evaluation until the namespace object's properties are first accessed. The module source is loaded and linked eagerly, but evaluation is deferred.
acceptanceCriteria: `import defer * as ns from "module"` syntax is parsed
Module is loaded and linked but NOT evaluated at import time
First property access on `ns` triggers module evaluation
Circular defer imports handled correctly
test262/test/language/import/import-defer/ — all tests pass (~84 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Implement AbstractModuleSource
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, `AbstractModuleSource` must be available as a constructor in the test262 harness (`$262.AbstractModuleSource`) for source-phase import tests.
acceptanceCriteria: `AbstractModuleSource` constructor is available
Prototype chain is set up correctly
test262/test/built-ins/AbstractModuleSource/ — all tests pass (~8 new passes)
No regressions

userStories.title: Fix remaining small categories
userStories.description: As a spec-compliant engine, all remaining scattered failures across small categories must be fixed: white-space handling, identifiers, line-terminators, global-code, function-code, types, directive-prologue, destructuring, keywords, punctuators, reserved-words, ASI, ThrowTypeError, JSON, StringIteratorPrototype, AsyncGeneratorFunction, GeneratorFunction.
acceptanceCriteria: language/white-space/ — all tests pass (~10 new passes)
language/identifiers/ — all tests pass (~8 new passes)
language/line-terminators/ — all tests pass (~8 new passes)
language/global-code/ — all tests pass (~14 new passes)
language/function-code/ — all tests pass (~5 new passes)
language/types/ — all tests pass (~4 new passes)
language/directive-prologue/ — all tests pass (~3 new passes)
language/destructuring/ — all tests pass (~1 new pass)
language/keywords/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
language/punctuators/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
language/reserved-words/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
language/asi/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
built-ins/ThrowTypeError/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
built-ins/JSON/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
built-ins/StringIteratorPrototype/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
built-ins/AsyncGeneratorFunction/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
built-ins/GeneratorFunction/ — all tests pass (~2 new passes)
No regressions
